original_question:
An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either red or blue, and there are at least $7$ marbles of each color. When $7$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $4$ of them are red equals the probability that exactly $5$ of them are red. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either green or yellow, and there are at least $8$ marbles of each color. When $8$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $5$ of them are green equals the probability that exactly $6$ of them are green. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.

An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either black or white, and there are at least $9$ marbles of each color. When $9$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $6$ of them are black equals the probability that exactly $7$ of them are black. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.

An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either purple or orange, and there are at least $10$ marbles of each color. When $10$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $7$ of them are purple equals the probability that exactly $8$ of them are purple. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.

An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either pink or gray, and there are at least $6$ marbles of each color. When $6$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $3$ of them are pink equals the probability that exactly $4$ of them are pink. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.

An urn contains $n$ marbles. Each marble is either teal or magenta, and there are at least $11$ marbles of each color. When $11$ marbles are drawn randomly from the urn without replacement, the probability that exactly $8$ of them are teal equals the probability that exactly $9$ of them are teal. Find the sum of the five least values of $n$ for which this is possible.
